concerns.text: FR-001 mandates downloading 'all ABX₃ entries' from the Materials Project API. The idea specifies querying for 'all entries labelled ABX₃' but the methodology sketch explicitly mentions retrieving data from 'Materials Project and the Open Quantum Materials Database (OQMD) releases'. The spec drops the OQMD data source entirely, creating an under-reach on the data acquisition scope defined in the idea.

User Story 2 acceptance scenario 1 states: 'Given a prepared training dataset with [deferred] of the data'. This is syntactically incomplete and fails to define the metric or reference for the deferred value. The spec template requires stating WHAT is measured and the REFERENCE, not just a placeholder. This violates the spec's own guidance on deferred markers.

User Story 3 acceptance scenario 1 states: 'Given a combinatorial library of [deferred] hypothetical ABX₃ formulas'. Similar to US-2, this fails to define the metric or reference. The idea explicitly targets '~5,000' formulas in the methodology sketch. While the spec defers the exact number, it must define the *source* of the enumeration (e.g., 'combinatorial substitution of A, B, X sites from defined element sets') rather than leaving the quantity undefined without context.

FR-004 requires generating '≥ 200 hypothetical ABX₃ compositions'. The idea's 'Expected results' section explicitly states: 'A ranked list of ≥ 200 hypothetical ABX₃ compositions... with the top 10 candidates will be flagged'. The spec correctly captures the 200 count but fails to explicitly mandate the 'top 10 flagged' requirement in the functional requirements, only mentioning it in SC-003. This is a minor under-reach in the FR section which should drive the implementation logic.
